2026-03-11T21:41:35.7204601Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T21:40:29.803411484Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":56986,\"title\":\"Azure.Identity: Support programmatic Transport and Policy customization for ConfigurableCredential and ChainedTokenCredential\",\"body\":\"Currently, credentials created via `IConfiguration` (through `ConfigurableCredential` and `ChainedTokenCredential`) are constructed entirely from config — there is no hook point for developers to programmatically set `Transport` or add `HttpPipelinePolicy` instances to credential options.\\n\\nThis is by design for the config-driven creation path, but creates a gap for scenarios where developers need to:\\n\\n1. **Override Transport** — e.g., inject a custom `HttpClientTransport` backed by `IHttpClientFactory`, or use a corporate proxy transport\\n2. **Add policies** — e.g., logging policies, telemetry policies, or custom retry policies that can\\u0026#39;t be expressed as config values\\n3. **Apply to chained credentials** — each source in a `ChainedTokenCredential` has its own options, so customizations need to reach individual credential instances inside the chain\\n\\n### Current Behavior\\n\\n- `ClientOptions(IConfigurationSection)` always creates a default transport via `HttpPipelineTransport.Create()` — config has no transport binding\\n- `HttpPipelineTransport` is abstract and cannot be instantiated from config\\n- `HttpPipelinePolicy` instances are added via `AddPolicy()` — a programmatic-only API\\n- `ChainedTokenCredentialFactory` creates credentials directly from `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` source elements with no parent propagation of Transport or policies\\n\\n### Desired Behavior\\n\\nProvide a mechanism for developers to customize Transport and/or add policies to credentials created from configuration. This should work for:\\n\\n- Single credentials (e.g., `CredentialSource: \\u0026#34;AzureCliCredential\\u0026#34;`)\\n- `ChainedTokenCredential` with multiple sources\\n\\n### Design Considerations\\n\\n- Should this be a callback/delegate on `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` that receives each credential\\u0026#39;s options before construction?\\n- Should there be a post-config `Action` that applies to all credentials in the chain?\\n- How does this interact with DI — should `IHttpClientFactory` integration be a first-class pattern?\\n- `Transport` is not serializable to JSON, so this is inherently a code + config hybrid scenario\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56986\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"Azure.Identity\"],\"assignees\":[\"m-nash\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-11T21:37:33Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-11T21:37:33Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

be expressed as config values\\n3. **Apply to chained credentials** — each source in a `ChainedTokenCredential` has its own options, so customizations need to reach individual credential instances inside the chain\\n\\n### Current Behavior\\n\\n- `ClientOptions(IConfigurationSection)` always creates a default transport via `HttpPipelineTransport.Create()` — config has no transport binding\\n- `HttpPipelineTransport` is abstract and cannot be instantiated from config\\n- `HttpPipelinePolicy` instances are added via `AddPolicy()` — a programmatic-only API\\n- `ChainedTokenCredentialFactory` 2026-03-11T21:41:35.7293666Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T21:41:16.828371493Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Type:** Feature Request\n**Component:** `Azure.Identity` — `ConfigurableCredential` / `ChainedTokenCredential`\n**Assigned to:** @m-nash\n\nThis is a well-scoped feature request to add a programmatic hook for customizing `Transport` and `HttpPipelinePolicy` instances on credentials built from `IConfiguration`. The core gap is that `HttpPipelineTransport` is abstract and cannot be deserialized from config, and `AddPolicy()` is inherently code-only — so a code+config hybrid mechanism is needed.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Summary of the Gap\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n| Area | Current Limitation |\n|---|---|\n| `Transport` | `HttpPipelineTransport.Create()` is always called; no config binding |\n| `HttpPipelinePolicy` | `AddPolicy()` is programmatic-only, no config path |\n| `ChainedTokenCredential` | Each credential in the chain has independent options; no parent propagation |\n| DI integration | No first-class `IHttpClientFactory` integration in the credential options path |\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e💡 Design Ideas \u0026 Considerations\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Callback/delegate on options** — A `Action\u003cTokenCredentialOptions\u003e` (or `Action\u003cTokenCredentialOptions, string credentialSourceName\u003e`) callback registered on `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` / `ChainedTokenCredentialOptions` that fires before each credential is constructed. This is the most flexible and DI-friendly option.\n\n2. **Post-config hook per source** — A dictionary-style API where callers register per-source customizers keyed by `CredentialSource` enum value. More granular but more verbose for \"apply to all\" scenarios.\n\n3. **`IConfigureOptions\u003cT\u003e` integration (DI)** — Leverage ASP.NET Core's options pattern so that `IConfigureOptions\u003cTokenCredentialOptions\u003e` implementations registered in DI are automatically applied. Natural fit for the `IHttpClientFactory` use case.\n\n4. **Propagation vs. per-credential** — For `ChainedTokenCredential`, decide whether the hook applies to all credentials uniformly (simpler) or per-credential-type (more control, but requires the callback to inspect the options type).\n\n**Recommended starting point:** An `Action\u003cTokenCredentialOptions\u003e` delegate property on `ConfigurableCredentialOptions` / `ChainedTokenCredentialOptions` that receives each credential's options object before the credential is instantiated. This covers Transport injection, policy addition, and is easily testable.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Relevant Code Locations\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/src/` — main credential implementations\n- `ClientOptions` constructor that accepts `IConfigurationSection` — where transport is hardcoded via `HttpPipelineTransport.Create()`\n- `ChainedTokenCredentialFactory` — where credential instances are created from `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` source elements\n- `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` — options class that would need the new callback property\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks / Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] **API design review** — Agree on the callback/delegate shape (`Action\u003cTokenCredentialOptions\u003e`, typed overloads, or DI-based)\n- [ ] **Design for single credential path** — Hook into `ClientOptions(IConfigurationSection)` to call the delegate before transport creation\n- [ ] **Design for `ChainedTokenCredential` path** — Propagate the delegate through `ChainedTokenCredentialFactory` to each credential's options\n- [ ] **IHttpClientFactory scenario validation** — Write a prototype that injects a transport backed by `IHttpClientFactory` via the new hook to validate the design works end-to-end\n- [ ] **Public API listing update** — Run `Export-API.ps1 identity` after implementing\n- [ ] **Unit tests** — Cover single credential, chained credential, and DI integration scenarios\n- [ ] **README / samples update** — Add a code sample showing the hybrid config+code customization pattern\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📚 Useful Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [Azure.Identity README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md)\n- [HttpPipeline and transport customization docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#transport)\n- [Azure SDK .NET Design Guidelines — pipeline policies](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-http-pipeline)\n- [IHttpClientFactory with Azure SDK blog post](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/net-framework-compatibility/)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n","item_number":56986},"name":"add_comment"}}}
